feed.title: Re: March 23rd 2021 Taproot Activation Meeting Notes

entry.title: Re: March 23rd 2021 Taproot Activation Meeting Notes
entry.summary:
Jeremy and Rusty Russell engaged in a discussion regarding the scheduling of a release, initially targeted for mid to late April with a start time around May 1st. The tradition of setting start and timeout times at midnight UTC was mentioned, with an aside about possibly changing it due to a thematic preference for the number 13, hinting at a Friday-the-13th inspiration. However, the technical focus shifted towards the estimation of when a specific block, block 695520, would be reached based on past data. This estimation is crucial for planning as it affects the timing of certain protocol actions.
Rusty Russell provided an analysis, noting that historically, reaching a similar count of blocks (19,440) has taken between 121 to 139 days, depending on the period considered. By examining the median time past (MTP) of block 676080, he suggested that selecting a median time no later than approximately 139 days later, or specifically 00:00 UTC on August 11th, would align most closely with the expected logic based on the height parameters previously discussed. This consideration takes into account the likelihood of blocks being found more frequently due to increasing hash rates, which could lead to the median time passing (MTP) meeting the desired conditions within the specified range of blocks if scheduled correctly.
Russell's communication underlines the importance of careful planning in the scheduling of blockchain-related events, taking into historical data and current trends in hash rate increases. This approach aims to maximize the probability that the MTP logic will match the height parameters set for the event, ensuring a smoother execution of the planned actions within the blockchain system.
